title: Platform Reliability & Security Operations
description: Acceptance scenarios for durable platform reliability and security operations packets spanning uptime posture, security operations, connector health, automation safety, incident readiness, release/change controls, and rollback evidence.
guardrails: The assistant never silently pages teams, closes incidents, changes degraded mode, alters runtime flags, changes traffic, rolls back deployments, or mutates production behavior.

The assistant never silently changes access, roles, tools, secrets, credentials, security exceptions, policies, automation state, connector configuration, ingestion runs, staging rows, prompts, models, or tenant rollout mode.

All platform operations, security response, connector retry, credential rotation, automation pause/enablement, and release/change actions remain approval-gated through the action queue and audit trail.

scenarios.name: Create platform reliability packet
expected: Creates an AssistantPlatformReliabilityPacket with reliability posture, security operations, connector health, automation safety, incident readiness, release/change controls, response plan, rollback plan, and leadership summary JSON.
Writes an assistant audit event with objectType assistant_platform_reliability_packet.

Does not mutate AssistantObservabilityIncident, AssistantPrivacySecurityTrustPacket, AssistantAiQualityReleasePacket, AssistantAdminControl, ApiHubConnector, ApiHubIngestionRun, AssistantAutomationPolicy, runtime flags, deployments, traffic, or security response records.

scenarios.name: Queue SRE/security review

body.note: Review API Hub auth failures before connector retry.

expected: Creates an AssistantActionQueueItem with actionKind platform_reliability_security_review and a link to /assistant/platform-reliability-security.
Updates only the packet status/actionQueueItemId to OPS_REVIEW_QUEUED.
Does not page teams, close incidents, rotate secrets, retry connectors, pause automation, change runtime flags, deploy releases, change traffic, or send security/customer communications.

scenarios.name: Approve reviewed platform packet

body.note: Approved for SRE follow-up; no source records changed.

expected: Marks only the AssistantPlatformReliabilityPacket as APPROVED with approver, timestamp, and note.
Writes an audit event proving the approval was human-reviewed.
Does not mutate incidents, access, secrets, connectors, ingestion, automations, rollout controls, runtime flags, deployments, traffic, or security/customer communications.
